codeReview.summary: Comprehensive integration test suite for workspace discovery covering all 6 validation assertions. 29 well-structured tests using assert_cmd and tempfile, following established patterns from other test files. Tests use isolated temp directories with proper HOME env var isolation for global fallback scenarios.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tests in discovery.rs use 6-character prefixes for ID resolution, while other tests in the codebase use 8-12 character prefixes. The prefix module requires minimum 4 characters, but the tests demonstrate that 6 chars works reliably for single tensions. Consider documenting the recommended prefix length guidance in AGENTS.md — currently it only states 'minimum 4 characters' without guidance on collision avoidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: werk-cli/tests/discovery.rs: uses &tension.id[..6] throughout; werk-cli/tests/add_show.rs: uses &tension.id[..12] for some tests. AGENTS.md states 'Minimum 4 characters' with no collision guidance.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test_no_workspace_error test checks for 'workspace' OR 'init' in stderr — this suggests the error message format may vary. The test could be more precise about expected error messages to ensure consistent UX.

summary: Feature 'foundation-discovery-integration' is a solid implementation of 29 integration tests covering all specified validation requirements. The tests verify workspace discovery from subdirectories (VAL-INIT-004), global fallback (VAL-INIT-005), consistency across commands (VAL-CROSS-004), error recovery (VAL-CROSS-007), prefix matching (VAL-CROSS-009), and first-use experience (VAL-CROSS-010). All tests use proper isolation via tempfile and assert_cmd. No blocking issues identified. Minor observations about prefix length conventions and error message consistency could be addressed in follow-up work but don't block this feature.
